g.text: · terminal Wi-Fi monitor for macOS with link health,
Live view of which AP / BSSID you're on, the BSSIDs around
you, connection latency / loss / jitter to the gateway and
WAN, an RSSI-variance environment monitor, and a deep BLE
device list — everything macOS hides from its own Wi-Fi
menu plus the diagnostic surfaces it never exposed.
* Tx and Max use different CoreWLA
current AP, signal bar, link / IP / radio details
every BSSID in range, grouped by physical AP
Link (gateway / WAN latency, loss, jitter) and
BSSIDs near you, or the BLE device list (toggle: n)
strip at the bottom; full browser via m
force a rescan now (CoreWLAN ~5 s throttle still applies)
cycle scan sort: by AP ↔ by signal
force re-roam (cycle Wi-Fi off/on so the OS re-picks the
toggle Nearby view: Wi-Fi BSSIDs ↔ BLE devices
open the Events browser (filterable list, per-AP σ
BLE list cursor — move selection up / down (BLE view only)
inspect the selected BLE row (open detail modal)
Filterable scroll of every event the dashboard has detected:
ROAM (AP switches), STIR (RF disturbance from σ baseline),
disassociate). Use 1/2/3/4/0 to filter by category. Below
the list: a per-AP σ table summarising which APs are stable
vs stirring, plus a σ sparkline covering the trailing hour.
Toggle with n. Two sections: Connected (system-paired
↑/↓/PgUp/PgDn to scroll · Esc or h to close
